extraction.one_line_summary: VINE Jay attacked solo from breakaway on final Cat-1 climb, took second stage win while EVENEPOEL Remco distanced ROGLIC Primoz in GC group sprint
tactical_patterns: VINE Jay attacked solo 1-2km from summit on Cat-1 finale, held 43s over chasers despite SOLER Marc closing to 20s at 700m.
EVENEPOEL Remco rode controlled tempo on finale rather than attacking, still gapped ROGLIC Primoz and YATES Simon by seconds.
JAY VINE secured breakaway after Category 2 opening climb eliminated sprinters, making time cut the secondary race dynamic.
LOPEZ Miguel Angel and AYUSO Juan chased in separate group 20-40s behind red jersey, revealing cracks in GC cohesion.

rider_signals.VINE Jay: positive: second stage win in three days from breakaway, finished 43s ahead despite late fade, emerging Grand Tour climber
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: controlled GC group tempo without attacking, still distanced ROGLIC and YATES, stronger than Stage 6 performance
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: negative: dropped by EVENEPOEL's tempo riding on finale, conceded seconds and fell to third overall at 1:01
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: positive: closed from 1:46 down to 20s behind VINE with 700m remaining, exceptional late-climb acceleration
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: negative: riding 20-40s behind red jersey group with LOPEZ, lost significant time despite white jersey position
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: distanced by EVENEPOEL on finale, losing contact with podium contenders on steep gradients
